notes:
SCOPE — PASS: All three deliverable files are inside src/renderer/game3d/assets/ or public/game3d-assets/; no files outside those trees. .gitkeep files confirmed at public/game3d-assets/models/, textures/, and audio/ via Glob.

STANDARDS — PASS: useScreenAssets.js is plain JS (no .ts/.tsx). Named export `useScreenAssets` and default export both present. Hook accepts a manifest param and returns { models, ready } as required. useGLTF.preload() is used for preloading and useGLTF.clear() is called in useEffect cleanup, so cleanup is properly wired. No package.json change detected (not in files_changed, git status shows package.json unrelated modifications predating this work).

RECEIPT ACCURACY — PASS: files_changed lists all six paths; all six exist on disk. exports_added matches the two export forms in useScreenAssets.js and the schema file. No discrepancies.

CODE QUALITY — PASS with one low-priority note: useGLTF() is called inside an array map on line 76 (paths.map(path => useGLTF(path))). This is flagged by the eslint-disable comment the worker added. The pattern is technically a hooks-rules violation (hooks in a loop), but the worker documents the constraint that the model count must be stable per mount — this is consistent with drei's own useGLTF constraints and is a common R3F pattern. Not blocking.

SCHEMA — PASS: valid JSON, draft-07, additionalProperties:true at root and every item level. screen_id is the only required field. Defaults provided for all arrays.

SAMPLE MANIFEST — PASS: proof-of-life.manifest.json conforms to schema (has screen_id, all optional arrays present and empty). _note field allowed by additionalProperties:true.

LOW: The hooks-in-map pattern on useScreenAssets.js:76 is a known constraint that callers must manage via key-prop remounting when model count changes. The worker documented this. Recommend a future cycle adds a runtime invariant check (e.g., warn if paths.length changes without remount) to surface misuse earlier.
